channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chel Diokno Disputes Bank Secrecy Claims Against Sara Duterte's Husband
item.description: House Assistant Minority Leader Chel Diokno of Akbayan party-list filed a counter-affidavit disputing Manases Carpio's allegations that he violated bank secrecy, anti-money laundering, and privacy law...

item.title: NSW government invests $12 billion to build new Tangara trains in Hunter Valley, creating thousands of jobs
item.description: The NSW government has announced a $12 billion investment over 15 years to construct a new manufacturing facility in the Hunter Valley for the next generation of Tangara trains. Two potential sites—Gl...

item.title: South African Recipes Inspired by the Springboks Team
item.description: The article highlights Pick n Pay's 'Eat Like a Champion' campaign, which collaborates with Springbok team dietitian Robyn Moore to provide accessible recipes for South African families. Three classic...

item.title: PDP Candidate Accuses State Government of Targeting His University with Tax Audit
item.description: Arthur-Jarvis Archibong, the PDP governorship candidate in Cross River State, accused the state government of using tax authorities to target his privately owned university, Arthur Jarvis University. ...

item.title: Cullie Ruto graduates with Bachelor’s degree in Interior Architecture from Heriot-Watt University Dubai
item.description: Cullie Chepng'etich Ruto, the daughter of Kenyan President William Ruto and First Lady Rachel Ruto, has successfully graduated from Heriot-Watt University’s Dubai campus with a Bachelor of Arts in Int...

item.title: Quadcopter crash reported in Islamabad's Humak locality
item.description: A quadcopter, flown from an unidentified location, crash-landed on the rooftop of a residential house in Islamabad's Humak locality. The incident occurred in Model Town, where a resident alerted the p...

item.title: Fluoride Contamination in Assam's Groundwater Leads to Severe Health Issues
item.description: The article highlights the severe health impacts of fluoride contamination in Assam's groundwater, particularly in districts like Nagaon, Hojai, and Karbi Anglong. Over 13 districts are affected, with...

item.title: Manitoba confirms EF1 tornado in Winnipeg
item.description: A tornado of EF1 strength has been confirmed in Winnipeg, Manitoba, according to the Northern Tornadoes Project. The storm, which was first reported on July 3, 2026, caused minor damage but no major i...

item.title: NDLEA Warns Against Scams Involving Fake Auctions of Forfeited Vehicles
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has issued a stern warning to Nigerians about fraudulent auction offers being circulated by scammers who impersonate senior officials. The agency clari...

item.title: Uganda's Political Transition: Power Struggles and Silent Leadership
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing political dynamics in Uganda, focusing on the return of President Yoweri Museveni's son-in-law, Odrek Rwabwogo, to frontline politics. Rwabwogo, who serves as a senio...

item.title: Political Dynamics and Health Alert in Western Australia
item.description: The article discusses political developments in Australia, focusing on the contrasting public personas of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and Treasurer Angus Taylor. Ben O'Shea analyzes Albanese's ren...

item.title: Quadcopter crash on house rooftop in Islamabad's Humak area
item.description: A quadcopter drone, flown from an unidentified location, crash-landed on the rooftop of a residential house in Islamabad's Humak locality. The incident occurred in Model Town, where a resident alerted...

item.title: Egypt's Infrastructure Development: High-Speed Rail and Nuclear Power Projects
item.description: The article discusses Egypt's ambitious infrastructure development plans, driven by foreign investment. Cairo is investing heavily in projects like high-speed rail, nuclear power, and new cities to mo...

item.title: India probes Venezuela over missing organs from deceased seafarer's body
item.description: Rakesh Chauhan, a 33-year-old Indian seafarer, died in Venezuela's Falcón state on May 7 after a heart attack, as per a Venezuelan medical certificate. However, an autopsy in Uttar Pradesh after his r...

item.title: Minimum Wage Hike in NCR: Assessing Its Impact on Families of Five
item.description: The Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) approved a P85 daily wage increase for minimum wage workers in Metro Manila (NCR), effective in two tranches. The first increase of P60 will take effect o...

item.title: Analysts' Forecasts and Recommendations for S&P/TSX SmallCap Stocks
item.description: The S&P/TSX SmallCap Index experienced a significant decline of 4.17% in June, underperforming the broader S&P/TSX Composite Index, which rose 0.25%. Despite this setback, the SmallCap Index remains t...

item.title: Missing Form 3 Student Found Hiding in 20-Year-Old Man's House
item.description: Patience Wambui Gitau, a 17-year-old Form Three student from St. Teresa Gatura Girls High School, was found hiding in the bathroom of a 20-year-old man's house in Kiambu County after being missing for...

item.title: The Guest List Explore Modern Masculinity and Unfulfillment in New Single
item.description: The Guest List, the Manchester indie band, have released their new single ‘If Ever Your Devil Is Kind’, which delves into themes of modern masculinity, unfulfillment, and societal pressures. The track...

item.title: PNP Prepares to Serve Arrest Warrant Against Senator Rodante Marcoleta Over Plunder Case
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has finalized protocols to serve an arrest warrant against Senator Rodante Marcoleta, following a plunder case filed by the Office of the Ombudsman. The case cente...

item.title: Legal Battle Over Forged Documents And Bribery Allegations Against Presidential Aide
item.description: Human rights lawyer Femi Falana (SAN) is set to defend Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi in a high-profile legal case involving allegations of forgery, bribery, and impersonation. Adeyemi accused Femi Gbajabiami...
